purpose: User-invocable forked-context Opus skill (`!eager_dispatch`). Runs a single comprehensive eager dispatch round on an active swarm: reads dashboard state, reconstructs the dependency graph, identifies all tasks with satisfied deps that haven't been dispatched, builds full worker prompts, and dispatches all simultaneously. Three phases: State Reconstruction (Steps 1-5: resolve dashboard, read state, read plan.json — regenerating if missing, read project context, build upstream cache), Identify Dispatchable (Step 6: completed/in-progress/failed sets, find ready tasks, present summary, await approval), Dispatch (Steps 7-9: launch agents, log dispatches, write master_state.json, report). Explicitly NOT a replacement for the !p_track execution loop — runs a single dispatch pass and exits.
gotchas: Single-pass: dispatches once and exits, does NOT monitor completions or run circuit breaker
Failed tasks are NOT auto-retried here — must use !retry {id}
If plan.json is missing/invalid, REGENERATES it via deep-thinking pass before dispatch (validate-plan-required.sh + validate-approval-gate.sh would block otherwise)
context: fork — runs in a forked Claude context; model: opus — uses Opus model
Dashboard resolution: SYNAPSE_DASHBOARD_ID env var > DASHBOARD ID directive > ask user; never scan
Shell preprocessing line at the top reads .synapse/project.json and dashboard state to inject runtime context
patterns: Forked-context skill: spawns a separate Claude instance for the work
Eager dispatch 5-step mechanism: completed set + in-progress set + failed set + dispatchable filter + parallel launch
Plan regeneration fallback: if plan.json missing, redo the deep-thinking pass before dispatch
conventions: Steps 1-9 numbered and grouped into 3 phases
